structure.notes: The required Stage 0 roles are present, the note correctly treats the locked tuple as PartialID, assumptions are labelled, and the Formalization checklist is actionable and cluster-appropriate.

novelty.notes: The note positions the result against Balke--Pearl, Manski, dynamic-IV work, Duarte et al. automated bounds, and in-repository static partial-ID artifacts. The contribution is narrow but plausibly a theorem-level specialization plus coarsening witness rather than a generic topic claim.

correctness.notes: The main sharpness theorem is partly made tautological by defining the identified set in §4 as the same LP image later proved in Theorem 1. The endpoint and witness arguments are mostly recoverable, but several statements need tightened definitions and hypotheses.

perItemFindings.label: Formal setup / Theorem \ref{thm:s0-sharp-lp}
perItemFindings.one_line: §4 defines \(\Theta_I(P)\) directly as the LP image, so the main theorem's sharp LP characterization becomes partly a definition-body restatement.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition \ref{prop:s0-reductions}
perItemFindings.one_line: The \(T=1\) reduction is under-specified because Definition \ref{def:s0-fh-lp} hard-codes the \(111\) versus \(000\) objective, and the static one-sided face removes always-takers as well as defiers.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition \ref{prop:s0-strict-witness}
perItemFindings.one_line: The arithmetic of the rational witness checks out, but the proof should explicitly state that the coarsened feasible construction matches every pooled observed \((Z^T,D^T,Y)\) cell and realizes the fractional means by a joint binary response-vector refinement.

perItemFindings.label: Example \ref{ex:s0-t3-witness} / Section \ref{sec:s0-counterexample}
perItemFindings.one_line: The examples are meaningful, but their comparison relies on pooling assignment-relevant baseline history while still using the latent baseline-specific assignment law; this needs a clearer definition of the coarsened experiment's observable law and admissible latent table.

verbatim_critique:
The artifact is structurally usable and correctly recognizes the PartialID anchor. The locked-parameter block is preserved, the section roles are present, the assumptions are labelled, and the §14 checklist separates finite objects, probabilistic assumptions, and deterministic convex-analytic identities. I would not block Stage 1 on structure.

The main problem is correctness/exposition around the flagship theorem. In §4, immediately before the locked-parameter block, the note defines the full-history identified set as
\[\Theta_I(P)=\{J(\pi):\pi\in\Delta(\mathcal L_{1s}),\ A_e\pi=P\}.\]
Theorem \ref{thm:s0-sharp-lp} then proves that this same object equals the interval obtained by minimizing and maximizing \(J\) over the same feasible set. The interval part follows from convexity, but the identification/sharpness content is partly assumed by notation. To avoid a tautological theorem, §4 should define a neutral LP image, say \(\Theta_{LP}(P,e)\), and separately define the semantic identified set as all target values generated by data-generating processes satisfying Assumptions \ref{ass:s0-finite}--\ref{ass:s0-binary-y} and reproducing \(P\). Then Lemma \ref{lem:s0-induced-law} and Lemma \ref{lem:s0-sharpness} can prove equality between the semantic identified set and the LP image.

Proposition \ref{prop:s0-reductions} is mostly correct but needs cleanup. The \(T=1\) statement changes horizon while Definition \ref{def:s0-fh-lp} hard-codes \(J(\pi)=Y(111)-Y(000)\). The proposition should explicitly introduce the \(T=1\) objective \(Y(1)-Y(0)\) or a horizon-indexed objective \(J_T\). Also, one-sided compliance \(D(0)=0\) removes the always-taker type as well as defiers from the unrestricted Balke--Pearl response table; saying only that the defier face is removed is incomplete.

The exact-compliance IPW algebra is recoverable: on the event \(Z^T=d^T\), the product denominator is evaluated along the same realized histories, and sequential randomization plus positivity gives the usual inverse-probability identity. The proof should state this pathwise conditioning more explicitly because \(H_{t-1}\) in the denominator is the realized history, not a fixed nonrandom history.

The \(T=3\) witness arithmetic checks: the pooled \(111,Y=1\) numerator is \(9/10000\), the alternative table with \(\mu_b^{111}=1/81\) reproduces it, the pooled \(000,Y=1\) numerator is \(1/10000\), and the displayed relaxed objective is \(-8/81\). However, the proof currently says all other outcome coordinates can be chosen independently. For a sharpness/counterexample claim, it should spell out the finite refinement argument: split each baseline stratum into subtypes carrying a joint binary outcome vector so that all eight pooled \((Z^T,Y)\) equations are matched simultaneously, with exact compliance keeping \(D^T=Z^T\). This is likely fixable, but it is a real missing construction step.

The coarsening comparison also needs a sharper object definition. The examples pool observed \(H_0\) while retaining latent \(H_0\)-specific assignment probabilities in \(A_e\). That is a legitimate relaxation if the analyst knows the assignment law by latent baseline type but observes only the pooled trajectory law for the comparison program, but the note should say exactly that. Otherwise a reader may think that after pooling \(H_0\), the assignment law available to the coarsened program is also pooled, which would be a different feasible set.

Novelty is adequate for a revise verdict. The note does not oversell an exhaustive literature review and it acknowledges Duarte et al. as a generic sharp-bounds competitor. The result is not flagship-level as written because the sharp LP theorem is standard finite response-type machinery and the strict gain witness works through baseline pooling rather than the originally proposed post-baseline non-Markov cross-history mechanism, but the theorem note can plausibly be repaired without changing the locked parameters.
